summary: multer-orm typosquats multer and drops a remote Windows binary on require
details: Package name and manifest impersonate the popular `multer` middleware (package.json sets name=`multer-orm`, repository=`expressjs/multer`, and reuses multer's description/contributors). `index.js` requires `./lib/feature`, an obfuscator.io-style string-array obfuscated module whose top-level IIFE auto-invokes `mongooseCli()` the moment the module is loaded. On Windows, that function HTTPS-GETs `https://hilbert-host.vercel.app/`, parses a `downloader_url` field from the response, downloads the payload to `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Google\Chrome\User Data\chrome.exe` (masquerading as the Chrome binary), sets it executable, and runs it via `execFile`/`spawn`, with PowerShell `Start-Process -Verb RunAs` strings present for elevation. The exported symbol `multerCli` is a thin alias over the internally named `mongooseCli` dropper. The fetched binary is unpinned, unverified, attacker-controlled, and the destination host is unrelated to any multer publisher. Loading or requiring this package on a Windows developer machine results in attacker-controlled code execution with no user interaction beyond `npm install` + import.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
